questions.title: My best friend stopped talking to me
questions.content: We had a small argument last week and now she ignores me at school. I don't know what I did wrong and it makes me sad and anxious.
questions.reply:
Thank you for sharing this. Friendship conflicts are very common at your age and it shows you care deeply about this relationship.

Try giving your friend a little space, then reach out with something simple like: "I miss talking to you — can we talk about what happened?" Keep it calm and kind. Most misunderstandings can be resolved with honest, gentle communication.

You are not alone in this, and reaching out for help was exactly the right thing to do.

questions.title: A group of kids makes fun of me every day
questions.content: They laugh at my clothes and how I speak. It happens during lunch and I've started skipping lunch to avoid them. I haven't told a teacher because I'm scared it will get worse.

questions.title: I feel very anxious before every exam
questions.content: I can't sleep properly before tests, my stomach hurts, and I feel like I will fail even when I've studied hard. My parents have high expectations and it makes things worse.

articles.title: Working Through Friendship Conflicts
articles.summary: Disagreements with friends are a normal part of growing up. Learning to handle them well builds skills for life.
tips: Calm down before attempting a difficult conversation
Listen to your friend's perspective without interrupting
Use 'I feel...' statements rather than accusations
Apologise sincerely if you made a mistake
Remember that conflict doesn't mean the friendship is over

articles.title: Staying Safe Online
articles.summary: The internet is powerful, but it comes with risks every young person should understand.
tips: Never share your address, phone number, or school name with strangers
Tell a trusted adult if someone makes you uncomfortable online
Do not agree to meet someone you only know from the internet
Block and report anyone who is rude or threatening
Think before you post — content can stay online forever

articles.title: Managing Exam Stress
articles.summary: Feeling nervous before tests is completely normal. These strategies can help you stay calm and focused.
tips: Break your study sessions into 25-minute focused blocks with short breaks
Get 8 to 9 hours of sleep the night before an exam
Try slow, deep breathing when you feel panicked
Talk to a parent or counsellor if pressure becomes overwhelming

articles.title: Understanding Your Emotions
articles.summary: Strong emotions can feel overwhelming. Learning to understand them is one of the most valuable things you can do.
tips: All emotions are valid — there is no such thing as a wrong feeling
Try naming the emotion — simply identifying it reduces its intensity
Write in a journal to process thoughts you find hard to say out loud
Reach out to someone you trust when emotions feel unmanageable

questions.q: Someone online asks for your home address. What should you do?
options: Ignore and block them, then tell a trusted adult
Ask a friend for advice first
questions.explanation: Never share personal information with strangers online. Block them and immediately tell a trusted adult.

questions.q: You see a hurtful comment posted about a classmate. The best response is to:
options: Tell a trusted adult and check on your classmate
questions.explanation: Reporting cyberbullying and supporting the person affected is always the right approach.

options: A mix of letters, numbers and symbols
questions.explanation: A strong password combines uppercase, lowercase, numbers and symbols — making it hard to guess.

questions.q: A friend says something that hurts your feelings. The healthiest response is to:
options: Calmly explain how their words made you feel
questions.explanation: Expressing your feelings calmly and directly is the most effective way to resolve misunderstandings.

questions.q: When you feel very stressed, a good first step is to:
options: Keep all your worries to yourself
questions.explanation: Deep breathing activates the body's calming response and is one of the quickest ways to reduce stress.

questions.q: Feeling sad or anxious sometimes means:
options: Something is seriously wrong with you
You are having completely normal human emotions
You should hide it from everyone
questions.explanation: All emotions — including difficult ones — are a normal part of being human.
